ops.body:
**Design round 1: draft REFUTED (2026-07-19). Core direction resolved; v2 in progress.**

The fable draft (Q1 holder-local mirror + StrikeStore.Snapshot; Q2 launchd-stays-relauncher; Q3 two-journals-joined) was rejected by adversarial (sol xhigh) review on a fundamental incompatibility:

FATAL CHAIN — the draft tried to keep launchd/open-W as relauncher WHILE adopting drain's Transition. But open -W cannot launch the successor until the OLD app exits, and drain.Transition requires the successor spawned BEFORE Run yields; Run won't yield until the successor is ready -> deadlock. A no-op SpawnSuccessor "durably lies" (records StepSpawn complete + releases the transition with no successor). And ordinary fusekit consumers independently open -n -g (mountd.Spawn/AppLaunchNew), so launchd is not the sole authority anyway.

RESOLVED DIRECTION (v2): cask retirement is a DEAD-OWNER-ADOPTION workflow, NOT a live Transition handoff. Old holder: Gate -> drain (settle + yield, canonical rows preserved) -> exit; launchd relaunches via open -W; new holder boots -> ScanPeers -> AdoptDead replays the dead generation. This keeps proc.Spawn out of the cask retire path (fork-storm safe) and is coherent with open -W. The dev/cask-less lane (proc.Spawn'd, gated) is the only Transition-eligible path.

The refute also corrected recon FACTS the v2 must re-ground: (F2) holder-retires.json is a bare []time.Time, not {times,level,parked_until} — level/parkedUntil are NOT persisted; (F3/F5) fusekit resets ladder level on process start + skew-clear while StrikeStore persists level+park with no reset -> ParkedUntil DOES change on the wire unless reset semantics are added; (F4) the threshold-strike change IS wire-observable (existing test pins 'third attempt: NO sweep'); (C1) Intake.Register admits only around the final Bump, not the whole mount op; (C3) lease.Fence.Release() returns an error so it does NOT implement drain.Fence (needs a composite adapter); (J5) the spec journal has only a process-local mutex, no flock. Full defect catalog (F1-F8, S1-S9, C1-C6, J1-J7, D1-D2) is in the workflow transcript; v2 addresses every BLOCKER/CRITICAL. Criterion 4d2ff5d stays PENDING until v2 passes a second adversarial pass.
